claim: Trump promises on Fox News and Lex Fridman's podcast to release the Epstein files if elected.
reality: Filed under commitments. Became the basis of congressional pressure after election.

claim: AG Pam Bondi tells Fox News the Epstein client list is 'sitting on my desk right now to review.'
reality: DOJ later stated no such list existed. Triggered congressional push for the EFTA.

claim: DOJ drops flight logs and a contact book that is mostly redacted. White House hands binders labelled 'Epstein Files: Phase 1 — Declassified' to conservative influencers.
reality: Much of the release was already public. Key names heavily redacted.

claim: Giuffre, Epstein's most prominent survivor-accuser, dies by suicide in Australia.
reality: Her FBI 302 interview records remain among those not released as of April 2026.

claim: DOJ declares the client list does not exist, no blackmail evidence found, death was suicide. Same day: DOJ fires Maurene Comey — the prosecutor who actually put Epstein behind bars.
reality: Directly contradicted by EFTA02857524 — a 205-page prosecution memo recommending 60-count indictment, withheld from the initial release.

claim: Trump: Epstein is 'somebody nobody cares about' (Jul 12) and the files are 'a big hoax' (Jul 16).
reality: The EFTA passed 427–1 weeks later, indicating 427 members of Congress disagreed.

claim: WSJ reports Epstein's 2003 birthday book contains a letter in Trump's name — text framed by a hand-drawn naked woman, signed 'Donald.'
reality: White House called it 'nonexistent.' Epstein's estate later produced the book to a congressional subpoena — matching WSJ's reporting in every detail.

claim: WSJ reports Bondi told Trump in May his name is 'among many' in the files. Trump denies it.
reality: A federal judge also denies grand jury release the same day.

claim: Maxwell interview released — she says Trump and Epstein were 'friendly in social settings.' Rep. Massie files discharge petition.
reality: Administration calls it 'not credible.' Discharge petition ultimately forces the EFTA.

claim: Epstein Files Transparency Act passes 427–1. Trump signs it, then posts calling it 'a Hoax' on Truth Social.
reality: 30-day statutory clock begins. Dec 19 deadline set.

claim: DOJ releases partial batch — 500+ pages entirely blacked out. Sixteen files vanish from the webpage within 24 hrs.
reality: Faulty PDF redactions allow public to read some names. Missed the statutory deadline.

claim: DOJ releases 3M+ pages, 2,000 videos, 180,000 images. Declares it 'final.'
reality: NPR later finds DOJ removed FBI records related to Trump accusations. A 6th release on Mar 5 backfills prosecution memos initially withheld as 'privileged.'

claim: NPR finds DOJ removed FBI records related to Trump accusations. 6th release on Mar 5 backfills prosecution memos withheld as 'privileged.'
reality: Only ~2% of total Epstein data released under Bondi's tenure. Bondi fired Apr 2 2026.

claim: Trump tells New York Magazine: 'I've known Jeff for fifteen years. Terrific guy... he likes beautiful women as much as I do, and many of them are on the younger side.'
reality: Open, on-record praise at the time of print. Flight logs and photographs place Trump and Epstein together repeatedly at Mar-a-Lago and on Epstein's jet throughout the 1990s.

claim: 'I had a falling out with him a long time ago. I don't think I've spoken to him for 15 years. I wasn't a fan.'
reality: Directly contradicts the 2002 'terrific guy' endorsement. Flight logs show joint travel as recently as 1997. Michael Wolff's 2017 audio captures Epstein describing Trump as 'my closest friend for 10 years.'

claim: 'I'd never been on Epstein's plane.'
reality: DOJ-released flight logs (December 2025) show Trump flew on Epstein's plane at least eight times between 1993 and 1996.

claim: Called the Epstein issue a 'Jeffrey Epstein Hoax' and a deflection tactic by Democrats.
reality: EFTA passed 427-1 five days later. The Epstein Files Transparency Act received near-unanimous bipartisan support.

claim: 'I know nothing about that' regarding Epstein emails. Called it a 'Democrat Hoax.' Snapped at reporter: 'Quiet, piggy.'
reality: WSJ had already reported in July 2025 that Bondi had privately told Trump his name appeared in the files.

claim: DOJ releases files showing Trump flew on Epstein's plane at least eight times between 1993 and 1996.
reality: Directly contradicts Trump's January 2024 Truth Social claim: 'I'd never been on Epstein's plane.'

claim: Christmas message calling others 'Sleazebags who loved Jeffrey Epstein' but insisting 'I was actually the only one who did drop Epstein... long before it became fashionable.'
reality: Flight logs show at least 8 shared flights 1993–1996. Wolff audio: Epstein calls Trump 'my closest friend for 10 years' in 2017.

claim: 'I have nothing to hide. I've been exonerated. I have nothing to do with Jeffrey Epstein.'
reality: FBI 302 records show Trump called Palm Beach Police Chief Reiter in July 2006 saying 'thank goodness you're stopping him.' EFTA01656152 lists Trump in FBI Prominent Names briefing alongside Dershowitz, Wexner, and others.

claim: Trump threatens to sue journalist Michael Wolff after release of audio tapes where Epstein claims: 'I was Donald's closest friend for 10 years.'
reality: Trump campaign calls it a 'false smear.' Audio submitted as congressional exhibit HHRG-119-JU08-20250227-SD006.

claim: Pam Bondi fired. Multiple sources confirm Epstein files handling was the primary cause.
reality: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles had acknowledged Bondi 'completely whiffed' on the Epstein files. Only ~2% of total Epstein data released under her tenure. House Oversight subpoena for Bondi testimony remains outstanding.

claim: THE NUCLEAR SITES IN IRAN ARE COMPLETELY DESTROYED!
reality: Directly contradicted by Feb 27 2026 press conference: Iran 'just can't quite get there' on renouncing nuclear weapons.

claim: 'They were close to getting a nuclear bomb. Absolutely.'
reality: Contradicts the June 24 claim that sites were 'completely destroyed' — if so, proximity to a bomb is moot.

claim: Threatened military action against Iran for killing protesters, then said killings stopped.
reality: Contradicted by reports showing death toll exceeding 7,000.

claim: Cited Iran's nuclear capability as justification for military action.
reality: Contradicts Trump's own prior statement that Iran's nuclear program was 'obliterated' after June 2025 strikes. Also contradicts US intelligence assessments.

claim: 8-minute Truth Social video announcing Operation Epic Fury.
reality: No congressional authorisation sought. War powers question raised immediately by members of both parties.

claim: 'Four to five weeks, but we have capability to do it in a day.'
reality: War entered week 8+ with no resolution. Four-to-five week claim abandoned without acknowledgement.

claim: 'It's over. As far as I'm concerned the war is over militarily.'
reality: Iran was simultaneously attacking US bases and allies. War continued 7+ weeks after this statement.

claim: War 'very complete.' Duration would be 'as long as it's necessary' / 'I feel it in my bones.'
reality: War was far from complete. Duration claim contradicted all prior estimates of four to five weeks.

claim: 'It'll be as long as it's necessary' and over 'when I feel it in my bones.'
reality: Abandons the firm 4–5 week timeline entirely with no acknowledgement.

claim: 'Iran is totally defeated and wants a deal — But not a deal that I would accept!'
reality: War continued for weeks after. Iran denied wanting any deal on these terms.

claim: Accused Iran of using AI to spread disinformation, claiming 'kamikaze boats' don't exist and a rally was 'totally AI generated.'
reality: Reuters verified real boats and real crowds.

claim: 'We've won militarily.' Rejected Pope Leo XIV's ceasefire call: 'you don't do a ceasefire when you're literally obliterating the other side.'
reality: Iran continued missile and drone strikes. Strait remained closed. Hezbollah resumed hostilities in Lebanon.

claim: Ultimatum: open the Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on power plants.
reality: Iran did not open the strait. Power plants were not immediately struck. Trump extended the deadline twice.

claim: Five-day pause on Iranian infrastructure strikes announced — 'productive talks.'
reality: Iranian official: 'no direct contact with Trump not even through intermediaries.' Iran called Trump 'deceitful.' No deal reached.

claim: 'Iran's New Regime President has just asked the United States for a CEASEFIRE!' Also: 'Regime change was not our goal.'
reality: Iran immediately called the ceasefire claim 'false and baseless.' Directly contradicts Feb 13 statement: regime change 'seems like the best thing that could happen.'

claim: 'Total destruction' threat. Also: 'negotiations going fine' with Iran 'negotiating in good faith.' Attributed war partly to Greenland tensions.
reality: Greenland attribution contradicts all other stated justifications. EC President Costa: escalation will not achieve ceasefire.

claim: Most extreme statement — genocide-adjacent threat against Iran.
reality: MTG called for 25th Amendment. House Minority Leader Jeffries: 'completely unhinged.' Ceasefire announced 90 minutes before deadline.

claim: Ceasefire announced — 'A big day for World Peace!'
reality: Strait remained closed. Iran attacked three ships in the Strait during the ceasefire. Trump later threatened to knock out all power plants and bridges.

claim: 'Iran has fully and completely opened the Strait of Hormuz.'
reality: Iran had NOT fully opened the strait. Naval blockade remained. Trump immediately contradicted himself by saying blockade would remain 'until a deal is fully signed.'

claim: Told CBS Iran 'agreed to everything.' Also told Bloomberg Iran agreed to indefinite suspension of its nuclear program.
reality: Iranian officials outright rejected his assertions within 48 hours. Own officials anonymously told WSJ and CNN his posts were hampering peace talks.

claim: Ceasefire violation claim + new threat: 'No more Mr. Nice Guy.'
reality: 'No more Mr. Nice Guy' directly contradicts 'Total and complete victory' declared just 12 days earlier.

claim: 'I'm not going to let them rush me' on duration. Revised timeline to open-ended.
reality: Trump himself said 'four to five weeks' on Mar 1. No acknowledgement of the contradiction.

claim: 'Oil is at historic lows. Nobody's making money.' Expressed optimism on peace deal.
reality: Goldman and IMF numbers directly contradict Trump's reassurance. Own officials anonymously told WSJ and CNN his Truth Social posts are hampering peace talks.

claim: Claimed U.S. is in 'complete control' of Strait of Hormuz and ordered Navy to 'shoot and kill any boat' laying mines.
reality: Issued during active ceasefire Trump himself extended 48 hours earlier. Iran ignored the order and attacked three ships.

claim: Regime change 'seems like that would be the best thing that could happen.'
reality: Directly contradicts Apr 1 statement: 'Regime change was not our goal.'

claim: Iran 'just can't quite get there' on renouncing nuclear weapons.
reality: Contradicts Jun 24 2025 claim that Iranian nuclear sites were 'completely destroyed.'

claim: Iran had missiles that 'would soon have had missiles capable of reaching our beautiful America.'
reality: Contradicts Jun 24 2025 'completely destroyed' claim — if destroyed, capability to threaten the US is eliminated.

claim: Trump announced a 90-day pause on tariffs. S&P 500 jumped almost 10% — one of its largest single-day gains since WWII.
reality: 18 minutes before the announcement, traders made large bets on the S&P 500 surging. Pattern flagged by Rep. Steven Horsford: 'That is a pattern.'

claim: Polymarket account 'Burdensome-Mix' bet $32,500 on Maduro leaving office before a US operation.
reality: Won $436,000. Changed username immediately. Stopped betting. Pattern structurally identical to Iran ceasefire bets made weeks later.

claim: Six new Polymarket accounts bet on a US strike on Iran.
reality: Earned ~$1.2M combined. One account earned another $163K betting on a ceasefire by April 7.

claim: 47 minutes before a CBS journalist tweeted about a Trump interview signaling the war was 'very complete,' large volume of oil futures bets appeared.
reality: Oil fell ~25%. The timing gap between bet and announcement is consistent with advance knowledge of the interview.

claim: $1.5B+ in S&P 500 futures and contracts for >6 million barrels of oil traded in 2 minutes.
reality: 16 minutes later, Trump posted halting strikes. Oil fell ~15%, stocks rose ~4%. Sen. Murphy: 'Who was it? Trump? A family member? A White House staffer? This is corruption.'

claim: Both Polymarket and Kalshi tightened insider-trading rules the same day as the futures spike.
reality: Donald Trump Jr. is an investor and adviser to both platforms. The simultaneous rule changes on the day of the spike were not publicly explained.

claim: At least 50 new Polymarket accounts bet on a ceasefire hours before the announcement.
reality: CFTC, SEC, and lawmakers called for investigations. Ceasefire was announced 90 minutes before Trump's self-imposed deadline.

claim: Rep. Steven Horsford: '18 minutes before last year's tariff pause... 47 minutes before the President's announcement on Iran negotiations, someone shorted the oil market by hundreds of millions of dollars. That is a pattern.'
reality: White House had warned staff not to bet on Iran war outcomes — suggesting awareness of the problem internally.

claim: BBC found spikes in defense and energy stock trading just before Trump's major speeches.
reality: The White House had warned staff not to bet on Iran war outcomes. SEC enforcement chief resigned. No criminal charges as of April 2026.

claim: Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr. have invested in drone companies competing for Pentagon contracts during Operation Epic Fury.
reality: No disclosure requirements applied. Pentagon contracts awarded without public tender process.

claim: Jared Kushner — Trump's son-in-law and one of his Iran envoys — is seeking to raise billions for his private equity firm Affinity Partners from Gulf states with direct interests in the Iran conflict outcome.
reality: Kushner participated in Iran negotiations while simultaneously fundraising from parties with strategic interests in the outcome.

claim: Trump convicted of 34 felony counts in Manhattan hush money case.
reality: Sentenced Jan 2025 to unconditional discharge. Seeking to move to federal court; judge ruled Trump waited 'too long.' Appeal ongoing.

claim: Federal election interference (Jan 6) and classified documents cases.
reality: Cases formally dismissed by Special Counsel Jack Smith following Trump's 2024 election victory.

claim: Trump appeals the $83.3M defamation judgment — E. Jean Carroll.
reality: 2nd Circuit upholds judgment in full, calling his conduct 'extraordinary and unprecedented.' Enforcement and further appeal possible.

claim: $464M civil fraud judgment — Letitia James lawsuit.
reality: NY Appeals Court slashes penalty to $0, though findings of fraud remain on the record.
source_name: CBS News / NY State Court Filings

claim: Trump v. Dow Jones · $20B defamation — filed day after WSJ birthday book story. Defendants include Rupert Murdoch personally.
reality: Assigned to Judge Darrin Gayles (Obama appointee). Trump had hoped for Judge Aileen Cannon — he did not get her. Murdoch told Trump he would 'take care of it.' He did not.

claim: Trump v. IRS · $10B suit over leaked tax returns.
reality: Judge questions whether a sitting president can sue the government. Hearing set for May 27, 2026.

claim: Trump asks NY's top court to vacate the $500M civil fraud judgment, calling it 'political hostility.'
reality: Original penalty slashed to $0 on earlier appeal, but fraud findings remain. New appeal seeks to erase findings.

claim: Jan. 6 civil lawsuits — immunity motion denied at discovery phase.
reality: Judge ordered Trump to explain by Apr 29 why discovery should not proceed.

claim: Freedom of the Press Foundation v. Trump — challenges OLC opinion declaring Presidential Records Act 'unconstitutional.'
reality: Ongoing. If upheld, would eliminate presidential records transparency obligations.

claim: Over 750 active lawsuits challenging executive orders and policies.
reality: Covers birthright citizenship, federal funding cuts, immigration enforcement, and agency eliminations.

claim: Rusk v. Trump, et al. — civil lawsuit against Trump, Marjorie Taylor Greene, and others.
reality: Open in 9th Circuit as of April 2026.

claim: Wolff v. Melania Trump — anti-SLAPP suit filed after Melania threatened Wolff with $1B defamation suit over Epstein audio tapes.
reality: Procedural stage — service dispute ongoing.

claim: GAO opens formal investigation into Epstein files handling and DOJ release process.
reality: Active as of April 2026. Bondi subpoena outstanding and unresolved at time of her firing.
